provenance.source_corpus: Terrestrial Insect Communities and the Restoration of an Industrially Perturbed Landscape: Assessing Success and Surrogacy

source_spans.text: Initial restoration efforts in these regions have focused on liming, seeding, and tree planting
increasing the plant diversity of these ecosystems
Restoration efforts were thus only moderately successful for reestablishing terrestrial insect communities
An increase in plant diversity does not necessarily result in an increase in insect diversity
